text: Marathon performance progressively slows as wet-bulb globe temperature (WBGT) increases from 5°C to 25°C.
Top male marathon finishers slow by 1.7% at WBGT 5-10°C, 2.5% at 10-15°C, 3.3% at 15-20°C, and 4.5% at 20-25°C compared to the course record.
Top female marathon finishers show a similar heat-related slowing trend (3.2% at 5-10°C, 3.2% at 10-15°C, 3.8% at 15-20°C, 5.4% at 20-25°C) but with greater variability.
Slower marathon runners experience greater performance decrements from heat than faster runners as WBGT increases.
The optimal WBGT range for marathon performance is 5-10°C, with meaningful performance degradation beginning above 10°C.
A WBGT increase from the coolest (5-10°C) to the warmest quartile (20-25°C) results in approximately 2.8 percentage points additional slowing for top male marathoners.
